summary: High‑quality soundboard recording captures a short but fiery 1985 show highlighted by scorching Tennessee Jed, Cumberland, Bucket, Miracle and a smoking Crazy Fingers.
review: The performance is widely praised for its energy – the second set opening with Tennessee Jed and Cumberland, plus standout renditions of The Bucket, Sugaree, El Paso, Dupree’s, Miracle and Crazy Fingers.  The soundboard source is generally clean, though a few listeners note tonal shifts from warm to bright, giving it an overall good‑to‑excellent listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (12 of 15) give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong musical moments; recording quality is described as good with only minor tonal variation, leading to a solid combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clean and balanced soundboard tape; some reviewers note the mix shifts between warm and bright tones but never becomes unlistenable. No major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is unusually short; the second set opens with Tennessee Jed and Cumberland, a surprise that many fans loved. Overall pacing is high‑energy with strong vocal rap moments (Phil/Brent) and a solid post‑drum jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone praised; he cuts off the jam to launch into Truckin'.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh delivers a notable vocal rap with Brent and sings along on several tunes.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) highlighted for his vocal contributions in the closing rap.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers receive praise for a powerful post‑drum jam; one reviewer notes a 27‑minute pre‑drum segment.
